task.objective: Implement Comparative Run Analysis that allows side-by-side comparison of two test executions to identify regressions, improvements, and changes
task.framework: Next.js 16 with App Router, Recharts, shadcn/ui

existing_patterns.pattern: Database queries use `getQueriesForOrg(organizationId)` wrapper pattern in lib/db.ts
API routes use `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` and auth check first
Components use `"use client"`, useState/useEffect for data fetching
Existing execution details in components/dashboard/execution-details.tsx for reference
Modal patterns from shadcn/ui Dialog component

dependencies.dependency: lucide-react - for icons (GitCompare, ArrowRight, ArrowUp, ArrowDown)
shadcn/ui components - Dialog, Select, Badge, Tabs

functional.requirement: Compare any two executions within the same organization
Show summary stats diff: pass rate delta, duration delta, test count changes
Categorize tests: new_failures, fixed, new_tests, removed_tests, unchanged
Display side-by-side test results with status changes highlighted
Support filtering by diff category (show only failures, only fixed, etc.)
Quick select recent executions for comparison

non_functional.requirement: Handle executions with thousands of tests efficiently
All data must be filtered by organization_id (multi-tenancy)

file.description: Add TypeScript interfaces for execution comparison
Add database function for execution comparison
Create API endpoint for execution comparison

constraints.avoid: Don't fetch full test results with artifacts - only status and duration needed for diff
Don't allow comparing executions from different organizations
Don't load unchanged tests by default - use pagination or filtering
constraints.prefer: Use FULL OUTER JOIN for accurate diff detection
Sort by severity (new failures first, then fixed, then others)
Use existing glass-card styling and color system

output_format.code: Implement each file following the code snippets provided
output_format.explanation: Implementation notes: 1. The comparison query uses FULL OUTER JOIN to detect new/removed tests 2. Tests are categorized by status change and duration change 3. The filter allows focusing on specific types of changes 4. Deep linking support via query params enables bookmarking
